---
title: "test"
format: html
editor: visual
---
  

# Set-up
```{r}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false

rm(list=ls())

library(readxl)
library(readr)
library(writexl)
library(openxlsx)
library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
library(ggplot2)
# library(ospsuite.utils)
# library(ospsuite)
library(esqlabsR)

# projpath <-  "G:/My Drive/Modeling_Projects/HT-PBPK/Preclinical_Study_Simulator/02_Scripts/"
# configurationPath <- file.path(projpath, "../ProjectConfiguration.xlsx")
# configurationFolderPath <- file.path(projpath, "../Configurations/")
```


#  (NOTE this is for a different type of molecule, not mAb, so parameters are different.)

## User Inputs
```{r}
#| echo: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false

## Input parameters
MoleculeId = "Test"
Lipophilicity = 1 #logunits
fu = 0.1 # plasma (fraction)
MW = 100  # g/mol
pKa = 10 # Acid
`Solubility at Ref pH` = 10000 # mg/L
`Ref pH` = 7.4
`Specific Intestinal Permeability` = 1E-05 # cm/min
`Liver Plasma Clearance- Dog` = 0.01 # mL/min/kg (dog, human)
`Liver Plasma Clearance- Human` = 0.01 # mL/min/kg (dog, human)
`Liver Plasma Clearance - Mouse` = 30 # mL/min/kg (mouse, rat)
`Liver Plasma Clearance - Rat` = 30 # mL/min/kg (mouse, rat)
`GFR fraction` = 1

## Single-dose simulations to perform
Species <- c("Mouse", "Mouse", "Rat", "Rat", "Dog","Dog") # Species to simulate
Route <- c("IV", "PO", "IV", "PO", "IV", "PO") # Routes to simulate (aligns with species)
Dose <- c(1, 10, 1, 10, 1, 10)
DoseUnits <- "mg/kg" # applies to all dose scenarios
SIM_TIME         <- "0, 24, 48;" #(format: "start, end, resolution; ...")
SIM_TIME_UNIT    <- "h"


#################### Pre-defined ####################
## For all PO doses
PO_WATER_VOL     <- 0.0035   # PO volume of water per body weight 
PO_WATER_VOL_UNIT <- "L/kg"  # PO water volume unit

## Protocol naming convention: {Dose}mpk_{Route}, e.g. "1mpk_IV", "10mpk_PO"
## (auto-derived in the Generate configurations chunk from Species/Route/Dose vectors)

# Molecule name in model
CONTAINER_PATH <- "GenericSmallMolecule"
```

# Generate configurations files
```{r}
#| echo: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false

# ---- 0. Helpers & paths -------------------------------------------------------
source(file.path(projpath, "myfuns.R"))

confpath <- file.path(projpath, "../Configurations")
poppath  <- file.path(confpath, "PopulationsCSV")

# Molecule identifier: used for ModelParameters sheet name and scenario naming.
# Set MoleculeId in User Inputs if needed; otherwise auto-detected from PhysChem file.
if (!exists("MoleculeId")) {
  pc_file    <- list.files(file.path(projpath, "../01_Data"), pattern = "^PhysChem_", full.names = FALSE)[1]
  MoleculeId <- if (!is.na(pc_file)) sub("^PhysChem_", "", tools::file_path_sans_ext(pc_file)) else "Compound"
}

# ---- 1. Simulation plan -------------------------------------------------------
# Zip parallel Species / Route / Dose vectors into a tidy data frame.
# ProtocolName is used as the sheet name in Applications.xlsx and referenced in Scenarios.xlsx.
sim_plan <- data.frame(
  Species      = Species,
  Route        = toupper(Route),
  Dose         = Dose,
  DoseUnits    = DoseUnits,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
) |>
  dplyr::mutate(ProtocolName = paste0(Dose, "mpk_", Route))

# ---- 2. Species meta ---------------------------------------------------------
# clh_suffix: suffix used in the model's CLh container name AND in the model file name.
# Extend or reorder rows here to add new species (NHP = Monkey in PK-Sim).
SPECIES_META <- data.frame(
  Species      = c("Mouse",  "Rat",    "Dog",    "NHP",      "Human"),
  PopulationId = c("Mouse",  "Rat",    "Dog",    "Monkey",   "Human"),
  IndividualId = c("Indiv1", "Indiv2", "Indiv3", "Indiv4",   "Indiv5"),
  clh_suffix   = c("Mouse",  "Rat",    "Dog",    "Monkey",   "Human"),  # matches CLh_* in .pkml
  pop_csv      = c("Mouse-Population.csv", "Rat-Population.csv",  "Dog-Population.csv",
                   "Monkey-Population.csv", "Human-Population.csv"),
  pop_skip     = c(2L, 2L, 2L, 0L, 0L),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
active_species <- SPECIES_META |> dplyr::filter(Species %in% unique(sim_plan$Species))

# Species → hepatic clearance value lookup (mL/min/kg).
# Variable names must match User Inputs exactly.
CLH_LOOKUP <- c(
  Mouse = `Liver Plasma Clearance - Mouse`,
  Rat   = `Liver Plasma Clearance - Rat`,
  Dog   = `Liver Plasma Clearance- Dog`,
  Human = `Liver Plasma Clearance- Human`
  # NHP = <add variable here if simulating NHP>
)

# ---- 3. ModelParameters.xlsx -------------------------------------------------
# Only physico-chemical parameters go here — shared identically across all species.
# Species-specific parameters (e.g., CLh) must NOT be placed here because esqlabsR
# applies every row to every scenario regardless of which model file is loaded,
# which causes "path not found" errors for species-specific containers.
mp_rows <- data.frame(
  ContainerPath = rep(CONTAINER_PATH, 7),
  ParameterName = c(
    "Lipophilicity",
    "Fraction unbound (plasma, reference value)",
    "Molecular weight",
    "pKa value 0",
    "Solubility at reference pH",
    "Reference pH",
    "Specific intestinal permeability (transcellular)"
  ),
  Value = c(Lipophilicity, fu, MW, pKa, `Solubility at Ref pH`, `Ref pH`,
            `Specific Intestinal Permeability`),
  Units = c("Log Units", "", "g/mol", "", "mg/L", "", "cm/min"),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

mp_wb <- openxlsx::createWorkbook()
# Global sheet: empty but required by esqlabsR
openxlsx::addWorksheet(mp_wb, "Global")
openxlsx::writeData(mp_wb, "Global",
  as.data.frame(t(c("Container Path", "Parameter Name", "Value", "Units"))),
  startRow = 1, colNames = FALSE
)
# Molecule sheet: header row written manually to preserve spaces, data below
openxlsx::addWorksheet(mp_wb, MoleculeId)
openxlsx::writeData(mp_wb, MoleculeId,
  as.data.frame(t(c("Container Path", "Parameter Name", "Value", "Units"))),
  startRow = 1, colNames = FALSE
)
openxlsx::writeData(mp_wb, MoleculeId, mp_rows, startRow = 2, colNames = FALSE)
openxlsx::saveWorkbook(mp_wb, file.path(confpath, "ModelParameters.xlsx"), overwrite = TRUE)
message("ModelParameters.xlsx written (", nrow(mp_rows), " parameters)")

# ---- 4. Individuals.xlsx -----------------------------------------------------
# One representative individual per species, drawn from population CSV row 1.
biometrics <- dplyr::bind_rows(lapply(seq_len(nrow(active_species)), function(i) {
  sm  <- active_species[i, ]
  pop <- read_pop(file.path(poppath, sm$pop_csv), skip_lines = sm$pop_skip) |> dplyr::slice(1)
  extract_biometrics(pop, sm$IndividualId, sm$PopulationId)
}))

indiv_wb <- openxlsx::createWorkbook()
openxlsx::addWorksheet(indiv_wb, "IndividualBiometrics")
openxlsx::writeData(indiv_wb, "IndividualBiometrics", biometrics, startRow = 1)

# Per-individual parameter sheets: species-specific CLh clearance lives here.
# esqlabsR applies each Indiv# sheet only to scenarios that reference that IndividualId,
# so Mouse CLh_Mouse goes in Indiv1, Rat CLh_Rat in Indiv2, etc. — no cross-species errors.
for (i in seq_len(nrow(active_species))) {
  sp  <- active_species[i, ]
  clh <- CLH_LOOKUP[sp$Species]

  indiv_params <- if (!is.na(clh)) {
    data.frame(
      ContainerPath = paste0(CONTAINER_PATH, "-Total Hepatic Clearance-CLh_", sp$clh_suffix),
      ParameterName = "Plasma clearance",
      Value         = clh,
      Units         = "mL/min/kg",
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  } else {
    data.frame(ContainerPath = character(), ParameterName = character(),
               Value = numeric(), Units = character(), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  }

  openxlsx::addWorksheet(indiv_wb, sp$IndividualId)
  openxlsx::writeData(indiv_wb, sp$IndividualId,
    as.data.frame(t(c("Container Path", "Parameter Name", "Value", "Units"))),
    startRow = 1, colNames = FALSE
  )
  if (nrow(indiv_params) > 0)
    openxlsx::writeData(indiv_wb, sp$IndividualId, indiv_params, startRow = 2, colNames = FALSE)
}
openxlsx::saveWorkbook(indiv_wb, file.path(confpath, "Individuals.xlsx"), overwrite = TRUE)
message("Individuals.xlsx written (", nrow(biometrics), " individuals)")

# ---- 5. Populations.xlsx -----------------------------------------------------
# Required by esqlabsR project configuration even when using individual-based sims.
# weightMin / weightMax are read directly from the population CSV files; leaving
# them NA causes a "Maximum value for MeanBW should be defined" error at runtime.

# Read population name AND weight range directly from each CSV.
# The `population` column in Demographics must be the PK-Sim internal population
# name stored in the CSV's "Population Name" column (e.g. "WhiteAmerican_NHANES_1997"
# for Human, "Mouse" for Mouse), NOT the generic label we use as PopulationId.
pop_stats <- dplyr::bind_rows(lapply(seq_len(nrow(active_species)), function(i) {
  sm  <- active_species[i, ]
  pop <- read_pop(file.path(poppath, sm$pop_csv), skip_lines = sm$pop_skip)
  bw  <- pop[["Organism|Weight [kg]"]]
  data.frame(
    PopulationId   = sm$PopulationId,
    pksim_pop_name = pop[["Population"]][1],   # PK-Sim internal name (e.g. "WhiteAmerican_NHANES_1997")
    n_individuals  = nrow(pop),
    weightMin      = min(bw, na.rm = TRUE),
    weightMax      = max(bw, na.rm = TRUE),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}))

demo <- dplyr::left_join(
  data.frame(
    PopulationName      = active_species$PopulationId,
    species             = active_species$Species,
    proportionOfFemales = 50,
    weightUnit          = "kg",
    heightMin = NA, heightMax = NA, heightUnit = NA,
    ageMin    = NA, ageMax    = NA,
    BMIMin    = NA, BMIMax    = NA, BMIUnit    = NA,
    `Protein Ontogenies` = NA,
    check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  ),
  pop_stats,
  by = c("PopulationName" = "PopulationId")
) |>
  dplyr::rename(population = pksim_pop_name, numberOfIndividuals = n_individuals) |>
  dplyr::select(PopulationName, species, population, numberOfIndividuals,
                proportionOfFemales, weightMin, weightMax, weightUnit,
                heightMin, heightMax, heightUnit, ageMin, ageMax,
                BMIMin, BMIMax, BMIUnit, `Protein Ontogenies`)

uv <- data.frame(
  `Container Path` = character(), `Parameter Name` = character(),
  Mean = character(), SD = character(), Distribution = character(),
  check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
openxlsx::write.xlsx(list(Demographics = demo, UserDefinedVariability = uv),
                     file = file.path(confpath, "Populations.xlsx"), overwrite = TRUE)
message("Populations.xlsx written — weight ranges: ",
        paste(demo$PopulationName, "[", round(demo$weightMin,3), "-",
              round(demo$weightMax,3), "kg]", collapse = ", "))

# ---- 6. Applications.xlsx ----------------------------------------------------
# One sheet per unique protocol (e.g., "1mpk_IV", "10mpk_PO").
# Sheet name must match ApplicationProtocol column in Scenarios.xlsx.
unique_protos <- dplyr::distinct(sim_plan, ProtocolName, Route, Dose, DoseUnits)

app_sheets <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(unique_protos)), function(i) {
  p     <- unique_protos[i, ]
  proto <- p$ProtocolName

  if (p$Route == "IV") {
    data.frame(
      `Container Path` = paste0("Events|", proto, "|Application_1|ProtocolSchemaItem"),
      `Parameter Name` = "DosePerBodyWeight",
      Value            = p$Dose,
      Units            = p$DoseUnits,
      check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  } else {
    data.frame(
      `Container Path` = paste0("Events|", proto, "|Dissolved|Application_1|ProtocolSchemaItem"),
      `Parameter Name` = c("DosePerBodyWeight", "Volume of water/body weight"),
      Value            = c(p$Dose,   PO_WATER_VOL),
      Units            = c(p$DoseUnits, PO_WATER_VOL_UNIT),
      check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  }
})
names(app_sheets) <- unique_protos$ProtocolName
openxlsx::write.xlsx(app_sheets, file = file.path(confpath, "Applications.xlsx"), overwrite = TRUE)
message("Applications.xlsx written (", nrow(unique_protos), " protocols: ",
        paste(names(app_sheets), collapse = ", "), ")")

# ---- 7. Scenarios.xlsx -------------------------------------------------------
# Model file is now species- AND route-specific: GenericSmallMolecule_{Route}_{Species}.pkml
scenario_rows <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(sim_plan)), function(i) {
  sp <- active_species |> dplyr::filter(Species == sim_plan$Species[i])
  if (nrow(sp) == 0) {
    warning("Species '", sim_plan$Species[i], "' not found in SPECIES_META — row skipped.")
    return(NULL)
  }
  data.frame(
    Scenario_name         = paste(MoleculeId, sp$Species, sim_plan$ProtocolName[i], sep = "_"),
    IndividualId          = sp$IndividualId,
    PopulationId          = sp$PopulationId,
    ReadPopulationFromCSV = FALSE,
    ModelParameterSheets  = paste0('"', MoleculeId, '"'),
    ApplicationProtocol   = sim_plan$ProtocolName[i],
    SimulationTime        = SIM_TIME,
    SimulationTimeUnit    = SIM_TIME_UNIT,
    SteadyState           = FALSE,
    SteadyStateTime       = NA_real_,
    SteadyStateTimeUnit   = NA_character_,
    ModelFile             = paste0("GenericSmallMolecule_", sim_plan$Route[i], "_", sp$clh_suffix, ".pkml"),
    OutputPathsIds        = "Plasma_Conc",
    stringsAsFactors      = FALSE
  )
})
all_scenarios <- dplyr::bind_rows(scenario_rows)

output_paths <- data.frame(
  OutputPathId = "Plasma_Conc",
  OutputPath   = paste0("Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|", CONTAINER_PATH,
                        "|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)"),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

openxlsx::write.xlsx(
  list(Scenarios = all_scenarios, OutputPaths = output_paths),
  file = file.path(confpath, "Scenarios.xlsx"), overwrite = TRUE
)
message("Scenarios.xlsx written (", nrow(all_scenarios), " scenarios)")
```

# Run simulations
```{r}
#| echo: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false

myProjectConfiguration <-
  createProjectConfiguration(configurationPath)

# Define which scenarios to run (NULL = run all; filter rows as needed)
Keepscenarios <- read.xlsx(file.path(confpath, "Scenarios.xlsx"), sheet = 1) %>%
  pull(Scenario_name)

scenarioNames <-  Keepscenarios

# Create `ScenarioConfiguration` objects from excel files
scenarioConfigurations <- readScenarioConfigurationFromExcel(
  scenarioNames = scenarioNames,
  projectConfiguration = myProjectConfiguration
)

# Create simulation scenarios defined in the excel files and apply the custom parameters
myScenarios <- createScenarios(
  scenarioConfigurations = scenarioConfigurations)

# Adjust simulation run options, if necessary.
simulationRunOptions <- ospsuite::SimulationRunOptions$new()
simulationRunOptions$checkForNegativeValues <- FALSE

# Run simulations
simulatedScenariosResults <- runScenarios(
  scenarios = myScenarios,
  simulationRunOptions = simulationRunOptions
)

# Save results and store the path to the results for later re-use
outputFolder <- saveScenarioResults(simulatedScenariosResults, myProjectConfiguration)

```

## Plot and compare with observed data
```{r}
#| echo: true
#| warning: false
#| message: false

outputFolder <- file.path(projpath, "../03_SimOutputs/SimulationResults/2026-06-04 20-07")

results <- read_all_results(file.path(outputFolder))

results <- process_results(
  results_df        = results,
  model_params_path = file.path(configurationFolderPath, "ModelParameters.xlsx")
)

# My manual function
obs_data <- NULL #read_observed_data(file.path(projpath, "Data"))


df <- results
    
ggplot(df, aes(x = Time_hr, y = Conc_ngml, color = Route)) +
      geom_line() +
      facet_wrap(~ Species, scales = "free_y") +
      labs(
        x = "Time (hr)",
        y = "Concentration (ng/mL)",
        color = "Route"
      ) +
      scale_color_viridis_d() +
      scale_y_log10() +
      theme_bw(base_size = 16) +  
      theme(legend.position  = "bottom") 
```
